title: Regex lookahead assertions should not be contradictory
description: Contradictory lookahead assertions in regular expressions make the pattern always fail to match, which is likely a mistake and reduces code clarity.
recommendation: Review and correct any contradictory lookahead assertions in regular expressions to ensure the pattern can match as intended.
impact: Patterns with contradictory lookaheads will never match, leading to dead code or logic errors.

examples.bad: Pattern.compile('(?=a)(?!a)') // This pattern can never match any string.
examples.good: Pattern.compile('(?=a)') // This pattern matches strings starting with 'a'.

checks.message: Review and correct any contradictory lookahead assertions in regular expressions to ensure the pattern can match as intended.
